string 2: *knocking*
Yes?{next}
*mumbling* Zzz...{next}
Don't bother me outside of working hours...{next}
Don't answer.{next}
I'm coming in.
Do you have a minute? I need to ask you something.
...All right. Sure.{next}
If it's not part of my mission, I'm not interested.
While we're here doing this, the world is coming apart.
I appreciate it. Come down to the living room when you're ready.
Oh, and don't tell Inori. She's not ready to know about all this yet.

...We should hurry—they're probably already waiting for us. 
I'll see you in the living room.

Hey, don't be like that. There may be something in this for
you, too. Can't you just hear me out?
Spare me the poor attempt at humor. I just want to talk for a bit.
Hey... Do I really hafta wear this all the time?

Yes, deal with it. People aren't exactly used to seeing
kids with horns.

You don't want to go causing a panic, right?
You're gonna have to wear that when we go out.

I have to say... it fits perfectly. Never thought anyone
would wear that stuff again...
Now then, it's about time to head out.
Head out? Head out where?{next}
How come you have clothes in that size...?
Will I get paid for working on my day off?

There's someone I'd like for you to meet—especially
the little half-pint, there.

She's not a bad person, I can promise you that much.
She's just a little... eccentric, that's all.
...I'd like it if you would just read the room on that one.
Anyway, I want us to make a little outing together today.

Wh, what? I can't do anything about that,
save that for your bosses.
Anyway... will you come along?
I'll be honest, half-pint. I'd like a sample of your data.

Inori isn't coming, by the way. One look at the half-pint
dressed like that and I bet she'd—

...Never mind. She'd lose her mind if she heard me
saying that.

We're supposed to meet up at Shinjuku Park.
Come on. Let's head on over.
...Hey, Misono.
I hear you've been pretty busy lately, Kuremi.
Anyway, I brought the "sample" I promised.
Hmm. This little boy, then?

I also brought a helpful friend who knows how to handle
phase electron lifeforms. Should net you some better data.

I've known this captain here for ages. Only guy in public safety
with a collaborative bone in his body.

Public safety is secretly gathering intel on phase electron
lifeforms, too, by the way.

When I worked there, they refused to give us the budget to look into
them no matter how much I bugged them.
...You're just terrible at office politics.
You can't even collect data properly without our organization's help.
Hey, I'm willing to share my precious sample data with you, aren't I?
If anyone should be grateful, it's you guys, don't you think?
Okay, half-pint... I need you to come with us now.

Don't worry. I won't let them lock you up and throw away
the key or anything. I'll hold them to their word.

But to keep a long story short, just know that this is all 
for Inori's sake.
Well, I don't really get what's going on here,
but if it's for Inori, I'll do whatever I can.
Ready when you are.
Fair enough. I'll leave the rest to the boss lady, then.
If you would...
Dr. Simmons! We're all set!
Okay, okay.
Well! How entirely wonderful!

I can't believe this is actually happening! You're really 
here, in the flesh! A real phase electron lifeform!
Is that... what you call us Digimon?
"Digimon," you say? How fascinating!

Truly remarkable! Well, no time to waste. Let's begin the
experiment at once!
Is everyone ready?
Sheesh. At least introduce yourself first!
Oops! Of course. How rude of me. I'm Dr. Simmons. Monica Simmons.
What are you planning to do?{next}
You're pretty forward, aren't you? I mean, we just met.
Okay, okay... Aren't you just the life of the party?

What? No one told you? I'm a research scientist with a particular
interest in Digimon.

I want to learn everything I can about their capabilities as living organisms.
Toward that end, I have various hypotheses I wish to test.
Only through the most humane of methods, naturally.

My apologies. I thought I knew my manners, but my intellectual
curiosity got the better of me.

Sometimes I get so caught up, my sense of etiquette seems to fly
right out the window.
I'll do my best to be more polite in the future.
Well, I've never been described like that before. Truly fascinating.

But right now, I'm afraid my focus needs to be on the Digimon.
I do apologize.

At any rate, we'll draw too much attention here. Let's move
to a larger space, and I'll explain everything there.

A scientist working for public safety, huh. If she's
accomplished, there must be some public info on her.

I'll see what I can find out about her. You'll hear from me 
as soon as I learn anything, so sit tight.

Okay, okay. Let us begin. Just take a deep breath and relax.
You've got nothing to worry about... It's a simple experiment.

All you have to do is attack these officers with the full 
extent of the capabilities you possess.

...And you're the one who gives the commands, are you? I ask
for your cooperation as well, then.

I can't guarantee it for sure, but with our prototype device
here, you shouldn't be able to deal any real damage to them.
So don't worry and definitely don't hold back! Let's get started!!

Good! Yes, that's it! Go on! They can take it! Show me more.
MORE, I tell you!

That's enough for today, Simmons. Our equipment's reaching 
its limits, and that prototype isn't cheap to run, either.

Worried about the budget, Captain? A bureaucrat through and 
through, aren't you.

I'm just trying to keep all parties happy. Come on, Simmons.
We're calling this thing off.
Very well...

I need to have a little talk with these people. You head on
home first, okay?
Oof!
Oops! I'm sorry!
...Huh?
Oh, you're a kid, too! In that case, tag! You're it!
Looks like they're having a game of tag.{next}
Do you wanna play with your fellow kids?{next}
Go catch those kids!{next}
If you're curious, go ahead, go play with them.
...Play?
Hey, what're you waiting for?! You're it, you know!
Chase after them, and tag one with your hand.
O-Okay, got it!
H-Here I come!
Well, I'll leave the rest to you.
*panting* Did I do it? Did I tag you right?
Wow, dude... you're fast! You're amazing!
Tag sure is... fun, but I'm... all worn out!
Hey, no fair! You can't quit while you're ahead!
You'd better come back tomorrow! We'll see who's the fastest then!
Tomorrow, then! You hear?! You'd better promise!
...Okay! I promise!
Hey! Let's go home!
I've gotta tell Inori all about this!
Let's come back tomorrow! And let's bring Inori!
You'd better! We'll see you here tomorrow!

Agent {player}, I've made an attempt at reassessing the
current situation.

Pay close attention to this Inori Misono and Aegiomon...
Aegiomon's evolution was seemingly triggered by Inori.

There's no record of humans ever affecting Digimon like 
that before. That alone makes those two an anomaly.

And Shinjuku is where the worldwide calamity began. It's hard
to believe these two facts could be completely unrelated.

Just think! Ever since you met this girl, the very principles of
time itself have been turned on their head.

Inori Misono... and Aegiomon. From this moment forward, 
they're to be recognized as class-A anomalies.
In other words, a subject of our investigation.

I expect you to make every effort to find out all you can about them.
Don't miss a single detail. I'm counting on you.
